info.title: Sprinklr API
info.description: Sprinklr is a unified customer experience management (Unified-CXM) platform. This specification covers core API categories including Messaging, Case Management, Publishing, Profile/Contact Management, and Webhooks.

post.description: Generates an OAuth 2.0 access token using client credentials or authorization code grant. The returned token is used as a Bearer token in subsequent API calls.
Refreshes an expired access token using a refresh token.

refresh_token.description: The refresh token issued with the original access token

post.description: Send an outbound message on a specified channel (e.g., Twitter, Facebook, WhatsApp, etc.). The message is routed through the configured account for that channel.
Send a reply to an existing inbound or outbound message. The reply is threaded to the original conversation.

get.description: Retrieve details of a specific message by its unique identifier.

delete.description: Delete a specific message by its ID. This may also delete the message from the source channel if supported.

post.description: Search for messages using various filters such as channel type, date range, account, and keywords.
Create a new support case. Cases track customer issues and can be assigned to agents, prioritized, and linked to conversations.

get.description: Retrieve the full details of a support case by its unique identifier.

put.description: Update an existing case. Supports updating status, priority, assignment, custom fields, and other case properties.

post.description: Search for cases using filters such as status, priority, assignee, date range, and custom field values.
Add an internal or external comment to an existing case.
Close an existing case, marking it as resolved.

resolution.description: Resolution note for closing the case

post.description: Create and optionally schedule a post for publishing to one or more social media channels. Supports text, images, videos, and links.

get.description: Retrieve the details of a specific post, including its status, scheduled time, and publishing results.

put.description: Update a post that has not yet been published. Allows modifying content, schedule, and target channels.

delete.description: Delete a scheduled or draft post. Published posts cannot be deleted through this endpoint.

post.description: Search for posts using filters such as status, channel, date range, and account.
Approve a post that is pending approval in the publishing workflow.
Create a new customer profile. Profiles unify customer identity across channels.

get.description: Retrieve details of a customer profile by its unique identifier.

put.description: Update an existing customer profile with new information.

post.description: Search for customer profiles using filters such as name, email, channel identifiers, and custom fields.
Merge a secondary profile into this profile, combining all associated data and channel identities.

parameters.name: profileId
parameters.description: Primary profile ID to merge into

secondaryProfileId.description: The profile ID to be merged into the primary profile

post.description: Register a new webhook to receive event notifications. Sprinklr will send HTTP POST callbacks to the specified URL when subscribed events occur.

get.description: Retrieve a list of all registered webhooks for the current account.
Retrieve details of a specific webhook registration.

put.description: Update an existing webhook registration, including its URL, events, and active status.

delete.description: Remove a webhook registration. The webhook will no longer receive event notifications.

post.description: Upload a media asset (image, video, document) for use in posts and messages.

get.description: Retrieve metadata for a specific uploaded asset.

bearerAuth.description: OAuth 2.0 Bearer token. Obtain via the /oauth/token endpoint.

apiKey.name: key
apiKey.description: API key provided by Sprinklr for your application.

BadRequest.description: Bad request - invalid parameters or request body

Unauthorized.description: Unauthorized - invalid or expired authentication credentials

refresh_token.description: Token used to obtain a new access token

fieldName.description: The custom field name or key

value.description: The value of the custom field (string, number, boolean, or array)

SearchFilter.description: A filter condition for search queries

Attachment.description: A media attachment on a message or post

assetId.description: Sprinklr asset ID if uploaded via the asset API

accountId.description: The Sprinklr account ID for the channel to send from

id.description: Channel-specific recipient identifier (e.g., phone number for WhatsApp, user ID for Messenger)

templateId.description: Pre-approved message template ID (required for some channels like WhatsApp)

templateParameters.description: Parameters to fill in the template placeholders

customFields.description: Custom field values to associate with the message

inReplyToMessageId.description: ID of the message being replied to

Message.description: A message object representing an inbound or outbound message

profileId.description: Sprinklr profile ID of the sender

caseId.description: Associated case ID if linked to a case

modifiedTime.description: Last modification timestamp in epoch milliseconds

assignedUserId.description: User ID of the assigned agent

assignedGroupId.description: Group/team ID to assign the case to

profileId.description: Customer profile ID associated with the case

messageId.description: Message ID that originated this case

Case.description: A support case tracking a customer issue

messageIds.description: IDs of messages linked to this case

modifiedTime.description: Last modification timestamp in epoch milliseconds

firstResponseTime.description: Time of first agent response in epoch milliseconds

visibility.description: Whether the comment is internal-only or visible to the customer

accountIds.description: Sprinklr account IDs to publish to

link.description: URL to include in the post

scheduledTime.description: Scheduled publish time in epoch milliseconds. If omitted, publishes immediately.

campaignId.description: Campaign to associate the post with

approvalRequired.description: Whether the post requires approval before publishing

publishedTime.description: Actual publish time in epoch milliseconds

channelPostId.description: The post ID on the source channel after publishing

channelPostUrl.description: URL of the published post on the channel

ChannelIdentifier.description: A customer identity on a specific channel

identifier.description: The channel-specific user identifier (e.g., Twitter handle, Facebook user ID)

profileUrl.description: URL of the customer's profile on the channel

caseCount.description: Number of cases associated with this profile

messageCount.description: Number of messages associated with this profile

url.description: The callback URL to receive webhook notifications

secret.description: Secret key used to sign webhook payloads for verification

Webhook.description: A webhook registration for receiving event notifications

active.description: Whether the webhook is currently active

lastTriggeredTime.description: Last time the webhook was triggered

tags.name: Messaging
tags.description: Send and receive messages across social and messaging channels

tags.name: Case Management
tags.description: Create, update, and query support cases

tags.name: Publishing
tags.description: Schedule and publish content to social channels

tags.name: Webhooks
tags.description: Set up and manage event notification webhooks
